summary:
The dental pulp receives its innervation primarily through branches originating from the trigeminal nerve via the lingual nerve. These findings are consistent across multiple reliable medical resources focusing on oral anatomy and neural transmission mechanisms for dentition-related sensations including pain perception. Agent1 concluded based on two pieces of retrieved information:

The dental pulp gets mainly supplied by nerves coming from parts of the trigeminal nerve, particularly those traveling alongside the lingual nerve towards the mouth area where they connect directly into the pulp tissues inside each tooth. Multiple authoritative texts confirm these details without suggesting any other cranial nerves play a direct role in pulpal sensation beyond providing supportive structures indirectly perhaps but not serving main functional innervations needed clinically speaking when assessing causes behind various types of tooth pains etc..
